conversations.say: In the voluntary case conference, your evidence establishes that someone used the service exit during the missing interval, but not who used it. I separate that observation from your preservation failure.
conversations.offer: I will use your bounded wording, protect uninvolved names, and permit review of any attributed quotation.
conversations.request: Confirm for Samira that neither the camera gap nor the retained physical record identifies Vale, then state any factual correction to my timeline.

conversations.say: Dee’s account leaves the service-exit user unidentified. Your recognized route feature makes voluntary withdrawal plausible, but the manufactured segment prevents treating that resemblance as a completed route. Neither account establishes Vale’s later location or condition.
conversations.offer: I will show the compromised segment, provide bounded timeline access, and exclude refuge details unrelated to distinguishing withdrawal from harm.
conversations.request: In the conference, identify only the personally recognized feature and confirm what your prior-disappearance knowledge cannot establish about this event.

conversations.say: After removing the manufactured segment, the supported remainder is narrow: Vale entered the neighborhood, an unidentified person used the service exit during an unpreserved interval, and an intervention could represent danger, escape, or concealment. The trail does not establish Vale’s fate.
conversations.offer: I will join your independent comparison and supply this corrected timeline if you attach its limits whenever discussing Vale’s route.
conversations.request: Publicly confirm that the timeline cannot prove homicide, voluntary withdrawal, or system control, and that my client’s identity remains unresolved.

public_statement: The protected case conference leaves one unresolved contradiction: Samira recognizes a feature from an earlier voluntary disappearance, while Dee’s physical record identifies only an unknown service-exit user, and the client-supplied segment connecting them appears manufactured. Neither witness can place Vale beyond the gap. I will report that the anonymous client sought a conclusion the case cannot support; I will not deliver a purchased verdict.

commitments.promise: Separate the preservation failure from the unidentified route use, protect uninvolved names, and allow review of attributed quotations.
Exchange bounded timeline access and identification of the compromised segment for a protected comparison of personally recognized route features, without seeking refuge details.
Provide the independently supported remainder of the timeline in exchange for attaching my correction and provisional limits to route claims.
Conduct the conference without payment, surprise questioning, or publication of names without consent, and record uncertainty rather than force agreement.

correction: The recognized disappearance feature, unidentified service-exit use, and automated intervention cannot be joined into a verified Vale route; the client-supplied link appears manufactured, and no witness establishes Vale’s later location or condition.
goal_progress: Obtained voluntary bounded accounts from the neighborhood-ledger, service-route, and Vale-confidant social knots.
Completed a protected comparison between Dee’s unidentified service-exit evidence and Samira’s personally recognized disappearance-route feature.
Labeled the manufactured client segment as the unresolved contradiction connecting otherwise incomplete accounts.
Concluded that the anonymous client’s identity remains unproved and refused to provide the definitive purchased answer.
reasoning_summary: Shared human production and several incompatible customers make a human mantle the strongest identity interpretation, though no sender is identified. The Vale route ends in unresolved gaps: withdrawal is plausible, harm remains possible, and intervention proves neither intent nor homicide. The defensible client report is that the requested conclusion cannot be supported.
